programmes.show_description: Air ambulances bring the emergency room to critically ill or injured people across the country, including a man in cardiac arrest and a woman who's been trampled by a foal.
In an idyllic Oxfordshire hamlet, the fish in the river keep dying. Why? David Thewlis and Jason Watkins lead in this real-life drama of victims, whistleblowers and water companies. (Ep1)
The drama continues as David Thewlis stars in a real-life story of pollution and criminal convictions that exposes how water companies have polluted our natural resources for so long. (Ep2)
The drama concludes as David Thewlis leads in a real-life story of pollution and criminal convictions that exposes how water companies have polluted our natural resources for so long. (Ep3)

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at lofty ambitions in Walthamstow in East London, where the Clayton-Fisher family totally transformed their dark and dingy attic.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Professional luthier Gary helps Simon O'Brien to restore an old bluegrass banjo.
Rex continues to explore his identity as secrets threaten to spill at the joint engagement party. Gemma faces a painful revelation. And Mercedes returns from rehab with a shocking proposal.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
The Norm Who Came in from the Cold: Norm hurts his back while painting Frasier and Lilith's apartment so Frasier lets him sleep on the sofa. Rebecca tests her mothering skills.
Ma's Little Maggie: Cliff's worried about his girlfriend meeting his mum, but they get along famously. Rebecca aims to improve Sam's fertility.
Unplanned Parenthood: Rebecca and Sam argue over what last name their future child should have, and look after Carla's unruly kids for a night.
Lucky Suit: Robert's ecstatic when he's recommended for an FBI job, until it becomes apparent that Marie's meddling may scupper his chances.
The Skit: At a party, Ray and Debra do an amusing impersonation of Frank and Marie. But when the older couple turn the tables on them, Ray and Debra don't find it so funny.
The Breakup Tape: When Debra finds a cassette that contains a message from Ray's college girlfriend, she wants to know why he's kept it for so many years.
The Unnatural: Frasier tries to stop Frederick from realising that he's a less-than-perfect father when he fills in on the radio station's baseball team.
Roz's Turn: When a spot opens up at the radio station for a new talk show host, Roz decides that it's time for her to take the mic.
Ham Radio: Frasier's attempt to produce a live, 1940s-style radio drama for KACL's 50th anniversary proves disastrous when Niles takes matters into his own hands.
Three Dates and a Breakup (Part 1/2): Frasier has dates with three very different women over one weekend. Meanwhile, Martin and Sherry consider splitting up.
Escape to Motor Mountain: The guys find a mountain of motorcycles, but can they get through the avalanche of booby traps? Plus: a stubborn collector and a warehouse mega-pick.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
VW Corrado: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue Ashley's 1992 VW Corrado, which has been rotting away since he fell ill. Can the boys bring some joy back to Ashley's life?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Crewe, Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet rock 'n' roller Billy, who lives in a house that's frozen in time in the 1950s.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Sarah and Ian met in Spain and now want a holiday home in south-east Lanzarote for themselves and their family to enjoy. Jean Johansson shows them five great finds.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: On the Costa Blanca, Abi and Darren's 'boogie wonderland' is hit by technical trouble. And Gordon and Shona finally take the reins of a diving business in Marbella.
The new run continues. Carl and Liz host the week's second visit, at their coaching inn in Cambridgeshire, where converted stables and Highland cows are on the menu (not literally).
The new run continues. In South Wales, Richard hosts at his pub, setting the bar high with terrific tartlets, sensational seabass and crowd-pleasing cheesecake. Can he hit bullseye? (Ep2/5)
Antonia and Ross love Ibiza. They've holidayed there many times, and now hope to find their own holiday home there. Can Lucy Alexander deliver their dream?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: New: Nando's vs KFC: Battle of...
programmes.show_description: ...the Brands: KFC and Nando's go head-to-head as Kate Quilton explores the battle for our taste buds in a fiery look at flavour, fandom and a billion-pound fight to win the chicken wars.
...Standing: Could you stay handcuffed to a stranger 24/7? Self-confessed prude Charlie is hooked to heavily tattooed Rob. Can Christian hairdresser Nina handle Sara's chatty nature? (Ep2)
On the Gogglebox: Britain's Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Stafford's Rite of Passage, and Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time.
This time it's Corrie, The Stolen Girl, The Piano, Mickey Rourke on Celebrity Big Brother, savoury cocktails on Saturday Kitchen, the News on Katy Perry in space, and more.
Retired artists Carole and Roger have crammed their nine-bed home with objects from around the world. Will they part with possessions to raise much-needed cash? (Ep2/3)
The final four contestants tackle a mind-boggling game that would make Einstein go cross-eyed, before the last two go head-to-head. Who will be crowned the UK's first Secret Genius? (Ep6/6)
El Cantito: Gordon helps a Puerto Rican restaurant in Yonkers, New York, which struggled when Covid hit. Appliances break, uncooked chicken is served... Can Gordon turn it around?
Jason Manford, Lou Sanders, Bella Hull and Green Party leader Zack Polanski join Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker for a satirical round-up of the past seven days.
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj throw themselves into the restoration of a derelict chapel in Pwllheli. But as they explore their new home, it seems they're not alone...
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon in Derbyshire and their oldest ever house-hunters, Margot and Henry, in Berkshire.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Bar Wars V: The Final Judgement: It's time for Cheers and Gary's Old Town Tavern's traditional Halloween prank war, but Gary calls it off because of his high blood pressure.
Where Have All the Floorboards Gone?: The gang are racked with guilt when their sporting hero loses his form after developing an obsession with Norm and Cliff's bar trivia.
Head Over Hill: Sam has yet another disagreement with restaurant owner John Hill and appoints Carla to deal with him. But instead of exacting revenge, she falls for him.
Talk to Your Daughter: Ray's ready to speak to Ally about the facts of life but discovers that she's now more interested in knowing the meaning of life.
A Vote for Debra: Debra decides to run for school president, but Ray isn't sure that he can handle the twins by himself during all those meetings, so he votes against her.
Call Me Mom: As Ray finally feels comfortable enough to call Debra's mother 'mom', he wants Debra to reciprocate with Marie.
Three Dates and a Breakup (Part 2/2): Frasier and Niles celebrate Sherry's departure with glee, but she won't give up on Martin that easily.
Daphne Hates Sherry: Daphne and Sherry just can't get on, so Daphne decides to stay at Niles's apartment. Naturally, Niles can't believe his luck.
Are You Being Served?: Frasier attempts to console a distraught Niles after Maris sends him their divorce papers.
Ask Me No Questions: When Niles asks his brother if he and Maris should stay together, Frasier is required to give his answer some careful and considered thought.
Everything Must Go: On the trail of two vintage Harley-Davidsons in Pennsylvania, Frank gets more than he bargained for when one bike collector takes a liking to him!
Channel 4's midday news summary.
Opel Manta GTE: In Rochdale, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue former amateur rally driver Alan's Opel Manta GTE. Can they re-ignite Alan's spark?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Maidenhead, Simon O'Brien falls in love with an old ship's carpenter's chest, which he wants to gently restore, while Henry Cole renovates a miniature Tether car.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
It's full steam ahead for train driver Wayne and train manager Kate, as Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £85k budget can buy in Pinar de Campoverde in Spain.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In France, new plans keep Andrew and Richard on their toes. Best friends Janey and Sharon start an adventure. And will the floor-fillers fall flat at Abi and Darren's disco?
The new run continues. The B&Bers visit Barbara and Elizabeth's boutique B&B in Norfolk, where the hosts try to dance away some doubts about grout with an old-school Wurlitzer organ.
The new run continues. In South Wales, Daffyd plans a nutritious vegetarian-ish dinner party, but kitchen chaos ensues. Will his dud pud ruin his chances of winning the grand? (Ep3/5)
Jane and James scour eastern Crete for an Aegean gem on a budget of £130k. Will a house packed full of charm or a villa with wrap-around views be the perfect place for them?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Sue and Richard's beautiful home in Mapperley is no longer suitable since Richard had a cycling accident. Can Kirstie make it accessible? Or should they list it?
The Butcher of Suburbia: The landmark series brings us the gripping inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman - and the killer's macabre efforts to conceal her death. (Ep1/2)
The Butcher of Suburbia: The suspect admits to the murder of a 74-year-old woman. But a deeply disturbing tale that shocks experienced detectives has only just begun. (Ep2/2)
Andrew's sustained a severe facial injury in an accident at work. Tony's been crushed by a forklift truck. And in resus, 61-year-old John keeps having sudden blackouts. But why?
